about.title: How Scores Are Calculated
about.intro: Every combo gets a Competitive Authority Score from 0 to 10, built mostly from tournament results: how it places (wins and finals carry more weight), how big the event was, and how widely it shows up across independent events — not just how often it is mentioned.
about.scoring.title: Scoring Method
about.scoring.p1: The score combines three pillars, each weighted by how reliable the source is (tournament databases count far more than opinions):
about.scoring.tournament: Tournament performance: placement-weighted (1st > 2nd > 3rd), scaled by event size and faded over time
about.scoring.expert: Meta presence: usage share and the number of independent events a combo appears in
about.scoring.youtube: Corroboration: how many distinct trusted sources back it, across languages
about.scoring.community: Opinion-only mentions (YouTube, Reddit, theory tier-lists) count, but cannot stand in for tournament proof
about.scoring.p2: Each pillar has diminishing returns, so a combo proven across many events ranks above one that fluked a single small win. The meta shifts fast, so recent results weigh more (75-day half-life).
about.factors.title: What Affects the Score
about.factors.mentions: Placement in tournaments (1st/2nd/3rd) and how often
about.factors.weight: Event size — a major win beats a small local
about.factors.recency: Recency — results fade with a 75-day half-life
about.factors.consensus: Independent events and players running the combo
about.factors.results: Reliability of each source (tournament DB vs opinion)
about.sources.title: Our Sources
about.sources.intro: We collect data from these community sources:
about.disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
about.disclaimer.text: Scores reflect competitive tournament data and community consensus, not absolute performance. The meta evolves constantly as new parts are released and strategies develop. Use scores as a starting point, and experiment to find what works best for your playstyle.
